Delta Pavonis.meta_notes: Phase 2 curated 2026-05-29 under curation-data-contract (Tier 1 host #1, rewrite of the rolled-back Delta Pav template). Radius/Teff/luminosity anchored on Rains et al. 2020 VLTI/PIONIER direct interferometry (theta_LD = 1.828 +/- 0.025 mas -> R 1.197 +/- 0.016 Rsun, Teff 5571 +/- 48 K, L 1.24 +/- 0.03 Lsun); the prior synthesis used Bruntt 2010 asteroseismic R and Gaia GSP-Phot Teff and falsely assumed no interferometry (dec -66 is reachable by VLTI/SUSI, only CHARA/NPOI cannot). Rains cross-checks Bruntt 2010 asteroseismic R 1.20 / L 1.22 within 1 sigma; the prior md mislabeled Bruntt's luminosity (1.22) as radius. Mass = Bruntt 2010 asteroseismology 1.07 +/- 0.13 (no binary orbit; Lund et al. 2025 TESS gives modern nu_max 2269.8 / Delta_nu 107.9 uHz but no published modeled M/R). [Fe/H] = Gomes da Silva 2021 AMBRE-HARPS +0.36 +/- 0.02 (Bensby 2014 +0.37, Bruntt 2010 +0.33 corroborate). Activity = Gomes da Silva 2021 HARPS log R'HK -5.13 (replaces Henry 1996 -4.999; very inactive old subgiant); Hunsch 1998 ROSAT log(Lx/Lbol) ~ -6.4. Age is a DOCUMENTED DIVERGENCE: Holmberg 2009 GCS III isochrone 9.3 Gyr (range 5.8-10.7) recommended as consistent with the very low activity + slow rotation, vs Bensby 2014 isochrone 4.9 Gyr (range 3.3-9.6); ranges overlap 5.8-9.6 -- Phase 3 must document this. Rotation: only v sin i ~ 1.7 km/s (Bruntt 2010) -> P_rot <~ 36 d (sin i upper bound); no measured period, so not recorded as a rotation_measurement. Gaia DR3 GSP-Phot/FLAME rejected (Teff 5437, [Fe/H] +0.03, age 0.59 Gyr biased for this bright metal-rich star). Disk: NON-DETECTION -- Eiroa 2013 DUNES classifies delta Pav as a non-excess source with L_dust/L_* < 5e-7 (PACS 70/100/160 um all consistent with photosphere); the prior 'Lawler & Tanner 2014' debris-disk entry (nonexistent paper, fabricated geometry) was removed from disks_curated 2026-05-29.

mass_measurements.reference: Torres et al. 2015 (total system mass Aa+Ab)

radius_measurements.reference: Torres et al. 2015 (Aa primary; Ab=8.83 R_sun)
Provencal et al. 2002 (mass-radius for DQZ WD)

Vega.meta_notes: Stellar Phase 2 curated 2026-05-30 under curation-data-contract (Tier 1 inversion fix). Vega is a POLE-ON RAPID ROTATOR (i ~5-6 deg) with strong gravity darkening, so polar and equatorial Teff/R differ substantially and a single value is an approximation. Primary anchor = Monnier et al. 2012 CHARA/MIRC imaging 'Concordance Model' (the most recent revision; argues earlier work overestimated gravity darkening; all values cache-verified from its Table 2): R_polar 2.418 +/- 0.012, R_equatorial 2.726 +/- 0.007 Rsun (recommended radius = equatorial, the larger extent; Phase 3 should treat the star as oblate, mean ~2.6 Rsun); T_polar 10070 +/- 90, T_equatorial 8910 +/- 130, surface-mean Teff 9360 +/- 90 K (recommended); bolometric L 47.2 +/- 2.0 Lsun (apparent, pole-on-inflated, 58.4 +/- 2.2); mass 2.15 (+0.10/-0.15) Msun; age 700 (+150/-75) Myr (rotation-aware Geneva tracks). The four gravity-darkened models disagree: equatorial Teff spans 7557 (Peterson 2006) - 8910 (Monnier); ages 455 Myr (Yoon 2010, non-rotating isochrones) - 700 Myr (Monnier, rotation-aware) -- DOCUMENTED DIVERGENCE, recommend Monnier. Yoon 2010 (no arXiv) alt: mass 2.135, R_pol 2.362/R_eq 2.818, T_pol 10059/T_eq 8152, age 455 Myr. [Fe/H] = -0.5 (lambda Boo surface depletion; Yoon Z ~0.008, Monnier adopts Z 0.006/[Fe/H] -0.5). Rotation P = 0.71 +/- 0.03 d (Petit 2010 spectropolarimetric magnetic detection; Alina 2012; consistent with Monnier v_eq+R), omega/omega_crit ~0.77-0.88, v sin i 22 km/s, v_eq ~195-236 km/s, gravity-darkening beta 0.231. ACTIVITY: log R'HK is NOT physically defined for an A0 V star (no convective dynamo/chromosphere) -- intentionally not recorded; X-ray is a non-detection, L_X < 2e25 erg/s (Pease, Drake & Kashyap 2006, 'the darkest bright star').

mass_measurements.reference: Yoon et al. 2010 (rotating model from interferometry)

radius_measurements.notes: Equatorial radius (gravity-darkened Concordance Model, Table 2). Polar radius is 2.418 +/- 0.012 Rsun; the star is oblate (pole-on).

mass_measurements.reference: Bouchaud et al. 2020 (rotating evolutionary fit to interferometry)

radius_measurements.reference: Monnier et al. 2007 (mean radius; equatorial 2.03 polar 1.63 Rsun)

Fomalhaut.meta_notes: Stellar Phase 2 curated 2026-05-30 under curation-data-contract (Tier 1 inversion fix). Mass/radius/Teff/luminosity/age all anchored on Mamajek 2012 (2012ApJ...754L..20M), the dedicated age paper that re-derives the fundamental parameters and all values are cache-verified from its text + Table 2: mass 1.92 +/- 0.02 Msun (mean of 4 evolutionary-track sets, protosolar composition), R 1.842 +/- 0.019 Rsun and Teff 8590 +/- 73 K (van Belle 1999 relation from Absil 2009 theta_LD 2.223 +/- 0.022 mas + Davis 2005 f_bol), L 16.63 +/- 0.48 Lsun (bolometric, Davis f_bol + van Leeuwen 2007 parallax), age 440 +/- 40 Myr (weighted mean of Fomalhaut A isochronal 450 +/- 40 and the TW PsA companion's gyro/Li/X-ray ages 400 +/- 70). RADIUS/Teff DIVERGENCE: Davis et al. 2005 give a smaller R 1.744 / hotter Teff 8819 because they did NOT subtract the resolved circumstellar K-band excess; Absil 2009 corrected for it (theta_LD 2.223 mas), so the Mamajek/Absil values are preferred. The angular diameter is mildly oblate (1.021, Absil 2009), gravity darkening is mild (not pole-on like Vega). [Fe/H] near-solar: Mamajek assumes protosolar (Z 0.017, motivated by the solar metallicity of companion TW PsA); Dunkin 1997 measured -0.03; A-star spectroscopic [Fe/H] is scattered/unreliable (Erspamer 2003 +0.43). ROTATION: only v sin i ~93 km/s (Royer 2007 compilation, 85-100 km/s spread); NO measured photometric period for the A-star (the 10.3 d period belongs to the K-dwarf companion TW PsA) -- not recorded. ACTIVITY: log R'HK is not physically defined for an A4 V star (no convective dynamo) and no standalone coronal X-ray detection of the A primary exists -- intentionally not recorded.

radius_measurements.notes: R from van Belle 1999 relation using Absil 2009 excess-corrected theta_LD 2.223 mas + Davis 2005 f_bol. Davis 2005 (uncorrected) gives a smaller R 1.744 / hotter Teff 8819.

Barnard's star.meta_notes: Linear propagation accumulates ~0.5 AU position error over 66 years from perspective acceleration (μ≈10,300 mas/yr, ϖ≈548 mas, RV≈−110 km/s). Within perturber tolerance for KSP. For sub-AU accuracy, override with JPL Horizons state vector at JD2433282.5.

radius_measurements.notes: R_interf via Boyajian et al. 2012b limb-darkened angular diameter + Gaia DR2 distance

tau Cet.meta_notes: Stellar Phase 2 curated 2026-05-29 under curation-data-contract (Tier 1 inversion fix). Radius = Korolik 2023 CHARA/MIRC-X interferometry (theta_LD 2.019 +/- 0.012 mas -> R 0.793 +/- 0.004 Rsun, recommended). Mass = Teixeira 2009 asteroseismology 0.783 +/- 0.012 Msun (mean density + interferometric radius; cache-verified); Korolik 2023 g*R^2 mass 0.69 +/- 0.09 (spectroscopic logg) is the alternative. Luminosity = Teixeira 2009 0.488 +/- 0.010 Lsun (M_V + bolometric correction). Teff = Korolik 2023 interferometric 5370 +/- 20 K (theta_LD + Boyajian 2013 Fbol); Korolik Table 1 adopts the spectroscopic 5320 +/- 40 K (Santos 2013 gives 5310 +/- 17). [Fe/H] = Santos 2013 -0.52 +/- 0.01 (very metal-poor; Bruntt 2010 -0.18 is an alpha-enhanced outlier). Activity = Gomes da Silva 2021 AMBRE-HARPS log R'HK -4.977 (very inactive; long-term declining trend + ~11 yr cycle); Mamajek 2008 -4.958 corroborates. Rotation: NO direct photometric period exists; Korolik 2023 gyrochronology gives 46 +/- 4 d (recorded method=unverified), Baliunas 1996 Ca II HK ~34 d. AGE IS A DOCUMENTED DIVERGENCE: isochrone ~10 Gyr (Di Folco 2004, adopted by Korolik 2023) recommended as consistent with the very low activity + slow rotation, vs activity-age 5.8 Gyr (Mamajek 2008); Korolik quotes a literature range 4.4-12.4 Gyr. Gomes da Silva 2021 isochrone age (0.028 Gyr) is a metal-poor-star fit failure, rejected. Suarez Mascareno 2015/2016 do NOT contain tau Cet (verified). --- PLANETS: Tau Cet e (Feng 2017 candidate) was reclassified to 'False Positive Planet' by NASA Exoplanet Archive on 2026-04-09, citing Figueira et al. 2025 ESPRESSO sub-10 cm/s RV non-detection (A&A 700, A174, 10.1051/0004-6361/202553869). DB retrieval 2026-05-22 correctly inherited e's absence. Figueira 2025 also reports that f/g/h are at or below ESPRESSO's detection floor; NEA still carries f/g/h as pl_controv_flag=1 (not False Positive), but the system is on retraction watch. Cultural reference: Tau Cet e = 'Adrian' in Andy Weir's Project Hail Mary (2021); see docs/reference/cultural-context.md.

radius_measurements.notes: Refining the Stellar Parameters of tau Ceti: a Pole-on Solar Analog. Most recent direct interferometric radius; supersedes Teixeira 2009 / Di Folco 2004 with new CHARA/MIRC-X 2021 data. Consistent within errors with Teixeira 2009 (0.793 +/- 0.004 R_sun).
radius_measurements.source_title: Korolik et al. 2023: Refining the Stellar Parameters of tau Ceti: a Pole-on Solar Analog
source_used_for: tau Cet mean radius (recommended, CHARA/MIRC-X interferometry)

radius_measurements.instrument: VLTI / VINCI (K-band, via Pijpers 2003 / Di Folco 2004 angular diameter)
radius_measurements.notes: Solar-like oscillations in the G8 V star tau Ceti. Interferometric radius adopted from Di Folco et al. 2007 (CHARA/FLUOR, theta_LD = 2.015 +/- 0.011 mas) combined with Hipparcos parallax; used together with asteroseismic mean density to derive M = 0.783 +/- 0.012 M_sun (the value adopted in this DB's mass entry, Feng 2017's reference).
radius_measurements.source_title: Teixeira et al. 2009: Solar-like oscillations in the G8 V star tau Ceti
source_used_for: tau Cet radius (alternative; asteroseismic + Di Folco 2007 angular diameter)
consistency cross-check with Feng 2017 mass (0.783 +/- 0.012 M_sun)

radius_measurements.reference: Pijpers et al. 2003 / Di Folco et al. 2004
radius_measurements.notes: Earlier VLTI/VINCI angular diameter (1-2% accuracy) used in Pijpers 2003 (2003A&A...406L..15P) to derive R = 0.773 +/- 0.004(int) +/- 0.02(ext) R_sun. Superseded by CHARA/FLUOR (Di Folco 2007) and CHARA/MIRC-X (Korolik 2023).

sources_extra.title: Di Folco et al. 2007: CHARA/FLUOR angular diameter of tau Ceti
used_for: angular diameter source for Teixeira 2009 radius (theta_LD = 2.015 +/- 0.011 mas)

TRAPPIST-1.meta_notes: Activity (X-ray): underlying observation is Wheatley et al. 2017 (XMM-Newton 2014-12-17, 30 ks). The Wheatley paper reports Lx/Lbol = 2-4 x 10^-4 as a range bracketing two spectral-model fits (APEC vs cemekl); we record this as log Lx/Lbol = -3.55 +/- 0.15 (mid + half log-range). Burgasser & Mamajek 2017 Table 1 row 'log10(L_X/L_bol)' gives a single derived value -3.52 +/- 0.17 dex citing the same Wheatley measurement, which is the recommended entry here. Activity (H-alpha): literature reports log L_Halpha/L_bol in the range -4.85 to -4.60 dex across four surveys (Gizis 2000, Reiners & Basri 2008, Barnes et al. 2014, Burgasser et al. 2015; collated in Burgasser & Mamajek 2017 Table 1 refs 5-8). No single-paper anchor recorded because the current activity schema (value_h_alpha_ew_angstrom) does not have a log-ratio key, and the multi-survey spread exceeds tier-3 single-value tolerance. Schema extension for value_log_l_halpha_lbol is a separate task.

AU Mic.meta_notes: Stellar Phase 2 curated 2026-05-30 under curation-data-contract (Tier 1 inversion fix; M dwarf regime, very different from the FGK hosts). AU Mic is a ~22 Myr Beta Pic moving group M1 Ve flare star. RADIUS IS A DOCUMENTED DIVERGENCE: the community-standard 0.75 Rsun traces to White et al. 2019, which exists ONLY as an unrefereed AAS conference abstract (no journal paper, no arXiv); the only refereed interferometric radius is Gallenne 2022 VLTI/PIONIER (theta_LD 0.825 +/- 0.033stat +/- 0.038sys mas -> R 0.862 +/- 0.052 Rsun), recommended per the contract refereed-interferometry priority. Donati 2023 infers R 0.78-0.82 from L+Teff (between the two, supporting the larger value). MASS DIVERGENCE: Plavchan 2020 PMS-isochrone 0.50 +/- 0.03 (community standard, propagated to all planet papers; Wittrock 2023 0.51 agrees) recommended over Donati 2023 Dartmouth-isochrone 0.60 +/- 0.04. Teff = Cristofari 2023 SPIRou atmospheric fit 3665 +/- 31 K ([M/H] +0.12 +/- 0.10, [alpha/Fe] 0.00 +/- 0.04, log g 4.52, with magnetic fields modelled); Donati 2023 V-I color gives 3700 +/- 70. Luminosity = Donati 2023 log(L/Lsun) = -0.99 +/- 0.01 -> 0.102 Lsun (M_bol 7.22 + BC + distance); Plavchan 2009 SED gives 0.09 +/- 0.02. Age = Mamajek & Bell 2014 Beta Pic MG 23 +/- 3 Myr (median of F/G-star isochrones 22 +/- 3 Myr reconciled with the Li-depletion-boundary age; the oft-cited ~12 Myr kinematic/expansion age is explicitly rejected). [M/H] +0.12 +/- 0.10 ~ [Fe/H] since [alpha/Fe]=0 (Cristofari 2023). Rotation = Plavchan 2020 TESS 4.86 +/- 0.01 d (Messina 2017 4.86 +/- 0.02, Klein 2021 / Donati 2023 4.83-4.86 d corroborate). Activity = Tsikoudi & Kellett 2000 ROSAT quiescent Lx 2.24e29 erg/s (0.1-2.4 keV); with L 0.102 Lsun (3.90e32 erg/s) this is log(Lx/Lbol) ~ -3.2, near the saturation limit for active M dwarfs. Strong magnetic field for the Phase 3 magnetic axis: small-scale <B> = 2.61 +/- 0.05 kG, large-scale 550 +/- 30 G (Donati 2023 SPIRou ZDI). NEXXUS (Schmitt & Liefke 2004) does NOT contain AU Mic (G/K sample) -- the '2.51e29 from NEXXUS' web claim is wrong; Tsikoudi & Kellett 2000 used instead.

radius_measurements.notes: theta_LD = 0.825 +/- 0.033(stat) +/- 0.038(sys) mas. Only refereed interferometric radius for AU Mic.
CHARA interferometry, the community-standard radius (adopted by Plavchan 2020) -- but published ONLY as an unrefereed AAS conference abstract, no journal paper or arXiv.
Adopted value; R inferred from L + Teff (their own derived R is 0.78 +/- 0.04). Sits between White 0.75 and Gallenne 0.862.

61 Vir.meta_notes: Stellar Phase 2 curated 2026-05-30 under curation-data-contract (Tier 1 inversion fix). Radius/Teff/luminosity/mass/age anchored on von Braun et al. 2014 (2014MNRAS.438.2413V) CHARA interferometry: theta_LD 1.073 +/- 0.005 mas (theta_UD 1.037) -> R 0.9867 +/- 0.0048 Rsun, F_bol 36.06e-8 erg/s/cm2 -> Teff 5538 +/- 13 K and L 0.8222 +/- 0.0033 Lsun; Y2 isochrones (input [Fe/H] +0.01) -> mass 0.93 Msun, age 8.6 Gyr. No asteroseismology exists. Prior unverified Vogt 2010 mass 0.942 / radius 0.963 superseded. Boyajian 2012/2013 do NOT contain 61 Vir (verified) -- von Braun is the sole interferometric source. [Fe/H] = Santos/Sousa 2013 -0.01 +/- 0.01 (Sousa 2008 -0.02 +/- 0.01; near-solar). Activity = Gomes da Silva 2021 AMBRE-HARPS log R'HK -5.013 (1251 spectra; inactive); Mamajek 2008 -5.001 corroborates; X-ray is faint, log(Lx/Lbol) ~ -6.9 (Wright 2011 compilation of ROSAT/Voges 1999) -- available for the Phase 3 activity axis but not recorded as a measurement here (VizieR-only). AGE IS A DOCUMENTED DIVERGENCE but all old: isochrone 8.6 (von Braun) / 7.69 +/- 1.44 (Gomes da Silva 2021) / 6.3 (Valenti & Fischer 2005) vs activity/gyrochronology 6.1-6.6 (Mamajek 2008); recommended the Gomes da Silva 7.69 +/- 1.44 whose range (~6.2-9.1 Gyr) brackets the spread. Rotation = Baliunas et al. 1996 Ca II HK chromospheric period 29 d (the original measurement, no arXiv; compiled by Wright 2011 and adopted by Vogt 2010).

mass_measurements.reference: Toledo Padr Oacute N et al. 2021

radius_measurements.reference: Toledo Padr Oacute N et al. 2021

Teegarden's Star.meta_notes: Interferometry impossible at ~0.032 mas angular diameter (Gaia parallax 261 mas, R 0.107 Rsun). Schweitzer 2019 radius via SED fitting. Dreizler 2024 mass/radius are evolutionary_model re-derivation (alternate). P_rot 96.2 d adopted by Dreizler 2024 from Lafarga et al. 2021 spectroscopic activity indices; no formal uncertainty quoted. Age >8 Gyr from kinematic (thick-disc) consistent with PARSEC Bayesian 7±3 Gyr; recommended = 7±3 Gyr (Zechmeister 2019). log(LX/Lbol) = -4.9 from Fuhrmeister 2025 XMM-Newton+Chandra quiescent detection, superseding Zechmeister 2019 upper limit <-4.23.

radius_measurements.notes: Interferometry impossible at ~0.032 mas; radius from SED fitting

HD 69830.meta_notes: Stellar Phase 2 curated 2026-05-30 under curation-data-contract (Tier 1 inversion fix). Radius/Teff/luminosity/mass/age/[Fe/H] all anchored on Tanner et al. 2015 (2015ApJ...800..115T), a dedicated stellar-parameters paper: CHARA interferometry resolved the star (theta_LD 0.674 +/- 0.014 mas -> R 0.9058 +/- 0.0190 Rsun), SED bolometric fit gives L 0.622 +/- 0.014 Lsun and Teff 5394 +/- 62 K (SME spectral Teff 5385 +/- 44 agrees to 9 K), SME spectroscopy gives [Fe/H] -0.04 +/- 0.03 and log g 4.49, Y2 isochrones give mass 0.863 +/- 0.043. No asteroseismology exists for this star. Prior unverified values (Lovis 2006 M 0.86, Rosenthal 2021 R 0.887) superseded by Tanner. [Fe/H] alternatives: Santos 2004 -0.03 +/- 0.04, Sousa 2008 -0.06 +/- 0.02 (all slightly sub-solar). Activity = Gomes da Silva 2021 AMBRE-HARPS log R'HK -4.999 (wmean; -5.013 median, 711 spectra; inactive). AGE IS A DOCUMENTED DIVERGENCE but all methods agree the star is OLD and rule out the youngest (~0.6-2 Gyr, Song 2000) ages: Tanner SME-isochrone 7.5 +/- 3 Gyr (recommended, the more precise of Tanner's two) vs Tanner HRD-isochrone 10.6 +/- 4 vs Mamajek & Hillenbrand 2008 activity/gyrochronology 5.7-6.1 Gyr; ranges overlap ~6-10 Gyr. Tanner notes the old age means the warm asteroid belt cannot be primordial (must be replenished; Beichman 2005). ROTATION: NO measured photometric period exists; only v sin i 0.8 +/- 0.5 km/s (Tanner 2015 SME) and an empirical-relation estimate (~41.7 d, CHES Bao 2024) -- not recorded as a rotation_measurement.

rotation_measurements.notes: Ca II H&K periodogram (Mount Wilson HK), grade=probable (significant in only 1 of 8 seasons); corroborated by activity-calibration P_calc~35 d and the long-rotation expectation from log R'HK~-5.0. method=unverified because a chromospheric-index period is outside the photometric/v_sin_i/ZDI schema taxonomy.

eps Eri.meta_notes: Phase 2 curated 2026-05-29 under curation-data-contract (Tier 1 inversion fix; replaces the prior Phase-1 unverified mass/radius). Radius/Teff/luminosity/mass anchored on Baines & Armstrong 2012 NOI interferometry (theta_LD 2.153 +/- 0.028 mas -> R 0.74 +/- 0.01 Rsun, Teff 5039 +/- 126 K via theta_LD + bolometric flux, L 0.32 +/- 0.03 Lsun, M 0.82 +/- 0.05 Msun via Yonsei-Yale isochrone); cross-checked by Di Folco 2007 CHARA/FLUOR theta_LD 2.126 +/- 0.014 mas (-> R ~0.73). No asteroseismic solution exists (the star is too active). Prior unverified values (Llop Sayson 2021 M 0.82, Rosenthal 2021 R 0.759) superseded by the interferometric anchor. [Fe/H] = Santos 2004 high-res spectroscopy -0.13 +/- 0.04 (Sousa 2008 -0.11 +/- 0.03, Gomes da Silva 2021 / Tsantaki 2013 -0.15 +/- 0.03 corroborate; all slightly sub-solar). Rotation = Croll 2006 MOST photometry, two spots P1 = 11.35 d / P2 = 11.55 d (differential rotation k ~ 0.09); Froehlich 2007 reanalysis confirms P1 = 11.348 +/- 0.037, P2 = 11.553 +/- 0.020 d, equatorial P ~ 11.2 d. Activity = Gomes da Silva 2021 AMBRE-HARPS log R'HK -4.496 (553 spectra; active K dwarf, well above the -4.9 inactive floor); X-ray Lx ~ 2e28 erg/s (XMM-Newton 0.2-2 keV, cycle-averaged, Coffaro 2020), combined with L = 0.32 Lsun (1.22e33 erg/s) giving log(Lx/Lbol) ~ -4.8; Ca II cycle 2.92 yr. AGE IS A DOCUMENTED DIVERGENCE: gyrochronology/activity indicators give a young age (Barnes 2007 gyrochronology 0.44 Gyr, adopted by Coffaro 2020; literature range 0.2-0.93 Gyr per Fuhrmann 2004 / Song 2000), recommended as consistent with the high activity + 11 d rotation, versus isochrone ages that are unreliable for cool MS dwarfs (Baines 2012 Y2 ~1 Gyr, the authors explicitly noting it is older than other studies; Valenti & Fischer 2005 ~2.6 Gyr; Gomes da Silva 2021 isochrone ~4.8 Gyr). Phase 3 must document the young-vs-isochrone split. Boyajian et al. 2012 does NOT contain eps Eri (verified absent in all VizieR tables) -- not cited despite its fame as an interferometric-radius sample.

radius_measurements.notes: Limb-darkened angular diameter; R ~0.73 Rsun from theta_LD + Hipparcos parallax, consistent with Baines 2012 NOI optical measurement.

mass_measurements.reference: Gonz Aacute Lez Aacute Lvarez et al. 2023

radius_measurements.reference: Gonz Aacute Lez Aacute Lvarez et al. 2023

mass_measurements.reference: Su Aacute Rez Mascare Ntilde O et al. 2017

radius_measurements.reference: Su Aacute Rez Mascare Ntilde O et al. 2017

40 Eridani A.meta_notes: Phase 2 verification 2026-05-29 (Agent A). Radius/Teff/luminosity recommended switched from Rains 2020 to Boyajian 2012 (both CHARA-class interferometry, Boyajian has tighter fractional uncertainty: 0.45% vs 0.75% on R; 0.27% vs 0.59% on Teff; 0.8% vs 2.5% on L; method-tier tiebreak per add-star skill). 40 Eri A confirmed in both samples (Rains 2020 Table 1 as star #7 of 16 southern; Boyajian 2012 sample as GJ 166A). Documented Ma 2018 vs Bond 2017 age divergence: Ma 2018 isochrone 6.9 +/- 4.7 Gyr from K-dwarf SED+PARSEC isochrone fit, while Bond 2017 IFMR-derived system-coeval ~1.8 Gyr from 40 Eri B white-dwarf progenitor (initial mass ~1.8 M_sun, MS lifetime 1.7 Gyr + 122 Myr cooling). Bond 2017 explicitly says 'earlier concerns about an excessive age...appear to be resolved' (Section 6.2). Phase 3 synthesis adopts ~1.8 Gyr in Decisions but documents both in Canonical alternatives. Ma 2018 [Fe/H] is -0.42 +/- 0.04 (not -0.29; that came from Diaz 2018); Ma 2018 alt added. Burrows 2024 reports rotation period as '~42 days' with no formal uncertainty stated in the paper - earlier DB had a fabricated +/-2.5 d; replaced with paper-stated value and no uncertainty key. Pre-Burrows P_rot literature is contradictory: Saar & Osten 1997 cite ~37-38 d, Diaz 2018 sees ~38 d in HARPS S-indices, and Burrows 2024 NEID activity analysis lands at ~42 d. Planet HD 26965 b (Ma 2018 'Vulcan') refuted by Burrows 2024 multi-instrument 16-year RV; not in planets_curated.json. No 2025/2026 post-Burrows confirmation or replacement candidate found.

age_measurements.reference: Bond et al. 2017 (IFMR-derived system-coeval from 40 Eri B WD progenitor)

rotation_measurements.reference: Burrows et al. 2024 (paper states ~42 d, no formal uncertainty)

40 Eridani B.meta_notes: Age: Bond 2017 §6.2 reports total age ~1.8 Gyr (cooling age 122 Myr + IFMR-derived progenitor lifetime 1.7 Gyr via Salaris+2009) with no formal sigma; the uncertainty_gyr=0.5 above is a curator estimate, not paper-stated. Spectral type DA2.9 from Gianninas, Bergeron & Ruiz 2011 (2011ApJ...743..138G, hereafter GBR11). Magnetic field: Landstreet & Bagnulo 2015 (2015A&A...580A.120L, DOI 10.1051/0004-6361/201526434) is the authoritative non-detection — high-sensitivity ESPaDOnS broad-band circular polarimetry sets an upper limit of ~250 G on any field, with mean noise sigma<Bz> ~ 85 G. Fabrika et al. 2003 (astro-ph/0006050, BSAO 55 publication) had earlier reported tentative variability with semi-amplitude ~4-5 kG and two candidate rotation periods (2h 25m, 5h 17m), but explicitly noted they could not 'determine firmly a real sole period'; the more sensitive Landstreet & Bagnulo 2015 measurement effectively refutes the kG-level detection, so no rotation entry is curated. Metallicity: Bond 2017 does not measure atmospheric [M/H]; Fabrika 2003 reports ultra-weak metal absorption lines but attributes them to circumstellar/interstellar gas (accretion from ISM and from dM4e companion 40 Eri C at ~10^-19 Msun/yr), not photospheric pollution. No DAZ-style atmospheric abundance analysis available. Activity (chromospheric): N/A for radiative-atmosphere DA WD — no convective envelope to host the Halpha/Ca II / log R'HK indicators meaningful for M and FGK dwarfs.

40 Eridani C.meta_notes: Mass: Mason, Hartkopf & Miles 2017 §3 (page 6) paper body and abstract both state 0.2041 +/- 0.0064 Msun for the M dwarf companion (mass sum 0.776 +/- 0.024 Msun split via Heintz 1974 mass ratio with the van Leeuwen 2007 Hipparcos parallax). Bond et al. 2017 lists 0.573 Msun for 40 Eri B while Mason 2017 lists 0.575 Msun from the same dynamics — paper-level disagreement at the per-mille level, both within 1 sigma. We adopt Bond 2017 for B (recommended) and Mason 2017 for C (recommended). Mann et al. 2015 also independently reports a semi-empirical M_K-based mass M = 0.222 +/- 0.022 Msun (Table 5 'Gl 166 C' row, observed) and a Dartmouth model-fit mass 0.233 +/- 0.011 Msun (Table 7 same row, inferred). Neither is recorded as a separate entry here; future schema-tier work could add the Table 5 value as recommended:false. Radius / Teff: Mann et al. 2015 reports BOTH an observed Table 5 row and a Dartmouth-inferred Table 7 row for 'Gl 166 C'. Table 5 (Parameters of All Stars in Our Sample, page 29) gives R = 0.274 +/- 0.011 Rsun and Teff = 3167 +/- 61 K (this is the row the DB uses). Table 7 (Inferred stellar evolution model parameters, page 36) gives R = 0.242 +/- 0.004 Rsun and Teff = 3362 +/- 18 K. The structured method fields 'spectroscopic_calibration' and 'low_res_spectroscopy' match the Table 5 derivation (M_K-band radius relation + BT-Settl atmosphere fit), so Table 5 values are the consistent paper actual. A short-lived 2026-05-29 mid-session edit briefly substituted the Table 7 model-fit values; a verification subagent caught the resulting method/value mismatch and the Table 5 values were restored in the immediate follow-up commit. Age: system-coeval with 40 Eri B via Bond 2017 §6.2 IFMR-derived progenitor lifetime ~1.8 Gyr; uncertainty 0.5 Gyr is a curator estimate (paper reports only '~1.8 Gyr'). Mann 2015 Table 7 best-fit age log(age)=9.44 (~2.75 Gyr) with sigma_age=0.60 dex is consistent within 1 sigma. Luminosity: Cifuentes et al. 2020 CARMENES catalog row Karmn J04153-076 / GJ 166 C VizieR-verified (J/A+A/642/A115 lookup, 2026-05-29 follow-up): L = 6.51 x 10^-3 Lsun (paper actual; sigma 0.13e-3 matches the catalog's ~2% relative precision quoted in abstract). Cifuentes also reports Teff = 3100 K (SED-photometry derivation), R = 0.2798 Rsun, M = 0.2712 Msun, log g = 5.5 — different method tier than Mann 2015 (Teff 3167 K spectroscopic) and Mason 2017 (mass 0.2041 binary_orbit dynamical). Recommended entries kept on the higher-tier methods (Mann 2015 for R/Teff via low_res_spectroscopy, Mason 2017 for mass via binary_orbit). Mann 2015 implied L = 4*pi*d^2 * F_bol from F_bol = 0.87259 x 10^-8 erg s^-1 cm^-2 and d = 5.04 pc yields ~6.9 x 10^-3 Lsun, the ~6% offset vs Cifuentes 6.51 x 10^-3 reflects the difference between Mann's spectroscopic-Teff F_bol integration and Cifuentes' multi-band SED integration with Gaia DR2 distance — within normal cross-method scatter. Metallicity: Mann et al. 2015 Table 5 reports a direct [Fe/H] = -0.21 +/- 0.08 dex for Gl 166 C (SNIFS R~1000-3000 optical spectra + the Mann 2013a low-res spectroscopic [Fe/H] calibration vs solar-type companions, see Mann 2015 §3 and §4). Consistent within 1.5 sigma with the 40 Eri A value -0.31 +/- 0.03 (Sousa et al. 2008). Earlier 'N/A, inherit from A' decision in the 2026-05-27 working notes was a false-negative; the entry is now recorded as recommended:true under method='low_res_spectroscopy'. Rotation: Shan et al. 2024 (CARMENES VII, 2024A&A...684A...9S, DOI 10.1051/0004-6361/202346794, arxiv 2401.09550) reports P_rot = 8.56 d for Karmn J04153-076 = GJ 166 C with spectroscopic method (CARMENES activity-indicator time series) and quality flag D = 'debated' (per paper Sect. 4: 27 of 166 periods are debated, 68 provisional). VizieR-verified (J/A+A/684/A9, 2026-05-29 follow-up). The earlier 2026-05-27 'Kemmer 2025 ~137 d' decision was a fabrication-pattern compound mistake: Shan 2024 is the correct paper (Y. Shan is first author, J. Kemmer is 22nd of 47 co-authors), the year is 2024 not 2025, and the value differs by ~16x. No rotation_measurements entry is curated because the paper itself classifies the period as debated; a future independent confirmation (e.g. TESS analysis or RV modulation) would convert this to a recommended:false (then :true) entry. Activity (H-alpha): 40 Eri C is the variable star DY Eri, an optically active flare star; multi-survey H-alpha equivalent-width values scatter over roughly pEW = -2 to -4 Angstrom across CARMENES / Schoefer / Newton samples, no single-paper anchor curated in this pass. Deferred.
